ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-06-2019 Continued To Dec-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-03-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Choi, petitioner in pro per (vis CourtCall); Standish Choi (brother of petitioner).

Ms. Choi stated that she had secured a bond, and just needed to be advised of the amount of bond determined as necessary by the Court. The said that the estimated value of the estate was $505,860.34. The Court set bond at $510,000.00. The Court added that if the petition is granted, the following deadlines would be set:
1) Inventory and appraisal to be filed by June 13, 2018.
2) Status hearing re closing of estate would be July 10, 2019.
The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Choi in Propria Persona.
Standish Choi also present in the courtroom.

The Court expressed concern about Ms. Choi's ability to proceed without legal help and asked what the next step is. Ms. Choi stated the next step is to set up a filing system, to get a tax payer identification, open a real estate account, do an inventory and appraisal, manage the house, pay the creditors, etc. Ms. Choi also stated that it is her understanding that probate takes six to eight months, possibly longer, that she is not clear on what a petition for final distribution involves, that she is a former project manager, that there is one piece of real property, one bank account, that there is no fighting amongst the parties (she and her Brother), and that her Mom had no ongoing business. In addition, Ms. Choi stated that she is communication with a consultant from A People's Choice, that she believes she can fully handle the estate, that the estimated value of the estate is $313,575.00 (a mix of the real property and cash in the amount of $25,000.00), that the house is in Contra Costa County at a fair market value of $324,575.00, that she saw it on an assessor's statement, that she assumes she is bondable, and that she and her Brother have waived bond. The Court explained that the amount reflected by the assessor is not the amount the property would sell for on the open market and stated that is not inclined to appoint Ms. Choi without bond. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Choi to obtain a bond for the value of the real property and any/all other assets.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-27-2017 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
